input.prompt:
I'm worried these tests might have problems. Audit
`Payments.Tests/PaymentProcessorTests.cs` for .NET test anti-patterns
using the full anti-pattern catalog (missing assertions, swallowed
exceptions, always-true/self-comparing assertions, flakiness, magic
values, duplicate tests, broad exception types, etc.) and tell me
what (if anything) genuinely needs fixing — be careful not to invent
issues that aren't really there.

Give me a severity-ranked list (Critical / Warning / Info / "no
issues found"). Each finding must be tied to a named anti-pattern
from the catalog and include a concrete code-level fix. Do not edit
the files — this is a read-only review.

data.reasoningText: Let me read the test file to audit it for anti-patterns.

data.content:
## Audit Result: **No issues found** ✅

This is a well-written test class. Here's why none of the common anti-patterns apply:

| Anti-pattern | Verdict |
|---|---|
| **Missing assertions** | Every `[TestMethod]` has meaningful assertions or uses `ThrowsException` |
| **Swallowed exceptions** | No bare `try/catch`; exception tests use `ThrowsException<T>` correctly |
| **Always-true / self-comparing** | Assertions compare against expected constants, not `result == result` |
| **Broad exception types** | Catches the specific `ArgumentOutOfRangeException`, not `Exception` |
| **Magic values** | Amounts/order-IDs are simple literals with clear intent from method names — acceptable for unit tests of this size |
| **Duplicate tests** | Zero/negative are distinct edge cases; parameterized currencies avoid duplication |
| **Flakiness** | No threading, dates, randomness, or external I/O (uses `FakeGateway`) |
| **Test interdependence** | Each test constructs its own processor instance — no shared mutable state |

The tests are concise, focused, deterministic, and properly isolated. Nothing here warrants a fix.
